auth.login_prompt: Enter the administrator password to continue
auth.connect_failed: Unable to connect to the panel service. Refresh and try again.
auth.session_expired: Your session has expired. Log in again.

common.request_cancelled: The request was cancelled. Try again.
common.request_timeout: The request timed out. Refresh the history later to check whether it completed.
common.service_busy: The panel service is busy. Try again later.
common.service_exception: The panel service returned an unexpected response ({{status}}). Check whether the service is running or refresh and try again.
common.network_error: Unable to connect to the panel service. Check whether the panel is running, the network is available, the HTTPS certificate is valid, and the access path is correct, then refresh and try again.

dashboard.site_resources_note: Aggregates CPU and memory usage for each site by scanning the system users of PHP-FPM processes. Refresh interval: 30 seconds.

alert.rule_remote_backup: Remote backup failed (remote backup must be enabled first)
alert.rate_limit_help: Alerts are sent at most once every 30 minutes. A recovery notification is sent after the issue is resolved.

extension.reset_confirm: Restore the default themes and plugins list? Current configuration will be overwritten. Continue?
extension.description: Default themes and plugins available when creating a new WordPress site. Enabled items are checked by default on the site creation page.
extension.name: Name

cron.wp_cron_replace_help: By default, WordPress only triggers scheduled tasks on page visits, such as scheduled posts and update checks. This is unreliable and consumes performance. This task calls wp-cron.php directly on a system Cron schedule, which is more reliable.
cron.wp_cron_config_help_prefix: After creation, the panel automatically adds
cron.wp_cron_config_help_suffix: to wp-config.php to disable WordPress built-in pseudo cron. When this task is deleted, and the site has no other WP Cron tasks, the setting is removed automatically.
cron.full_backup_help: : packages the entire site root directory. It runs on the first execution or when manually executed from the detail page.
cron.smart_incremental_help: : only backs up files added or modified in wp-content/uploads/ since the previous backup. This mode is recommended for daily scheduled runs.
cron.incremental_keep_help: Incremental backups always keep the latest complete local chain and are not cleaned automatically.
cron.cron_expression_placeholder: Or enter a standard Cron expression (minute hour day month weekday)

software.log_empty_or_unreadable: The log is empty or cannot be read
software.invalid_action: Invalid action. Only start/stop/restart are supported
software.php_size_invalid: PHP size settings only support numbers or numbers with K/M/G, for example 128M
software.php_int_invalid: PHP time and variable count settings only support non-negative integers
software.nginx_value_no_semicolon: Nginx configuration values cannot contain semicolons
software.create_php_config_failed: Failed to create PHP configuration file
software.php_pool_rebuild_failed: PHP configuration was saved, but some site PHP-FPM pools failed to rebuild: {{error}}
software.max_input_time_hint: The longest time allowed for PHP to parse POST/upload input. The panel defaults to 300 seconds. Keep it aligned with max_execution_time for large uploads or imports.
software.memory_limit_hint: Maximum memory for a single PHP process. 128M for a simple blog, 256M for multi-plugin sites, 512M for WooCommerce/Elementor.
software.upload_max_filesize_hint: Theme, plugin, and media upload limit. Keep it aligned with Nginx client_max_body_size.
software.post_max_size_hint: Should be at least upload_max_filesize, otherwise large uploads may be blocked by the POST limit.
software.max_execution_time_hint: Maximum runtime for PHP scripts. Use 300+ for demo imports and batch jobs.
software.max_input_vars_hint: Use 2000+ for menus with many items or Elementor/Divi, and 5000 for large sites.
software.client_max_body_size_hint: Keep it aligned with PHP upload_max_filesize. Increase it for large theme or backup imports.
software.innodb_buffer_pool_size_hint: A conservative recommendation is 10% to 25% of physical memory. Use 128M for 1 GB, 256M for 2 GB, 512M for 4 GB, and 1G+ for 8 GB or more.
software.maxmemory_hint: Redis object cache limit. Use 128mb for a single WordPress site, or 256mb+ for multiple sites or high traffic.
software.process_guard_help: systemd Restart=always provides the first guard layer. The panel checks for abnormal services every 30 seconds and starts them automatically. After a manual stop, the guard will not start the service automatically.

ai_diagnostics.description: Select a website and issue type to run a read-only diagnosis. Files, databases, and server configuration are not modified automatically.
ai_diagnostics.privacy_notice: The diagnostic context is sent to the AI provider configured in panel settings. It may include the domain, log summary, configuration summary, and recent operations. It does not send database passwords, AI keys, SSL private keys, panel passwords, or panel database contents.
ai_diagnostics.timeout_help: If the page times out, the diagnosis may still be running in the backend. Refresh the history later to check the result.
ai_diagnostics.feedback_help: If an answer is inaccurate, invents panel entry paths, or makes clearly unreasonable judgments, send me a diagnostic screenshot or problem description.
ai_diagnostics.select_site_for_history: Select a website to view history
ai_diagnostics.needs_more_info: AI thinks the current information is insufficient. Add more information according to the suggestions above and continue the conversation below.
ai_diagnostics.followup_help: After sending, the current site status is collected again and sent with this session context.
ai_diagnostics.empty_chat_help: After diagnosis completes, describe what you did or paste new error information below.
ai_diagnostics.followup_placeholder: Example: I have enabled debug mode. Please check the current status again.
ai_diagnostics.followup_context_help: Continuing the conversation keeps the original diagnosis and chat history, so you do not need to start a new diagnosis.
ai_diagnostics.waiting_long: Still running, taking longer than usual...
ai_diagnostics.already_running_followup: A follow-up diagnosis is already running
ai_diagnostics.followup_wait: Please wait before sending another follow-up
ai_diagnostics.user_error_bad_response: Unexpected response from the AI service
ai_diagnostics.user_error_empty_response: The AI service returned an empty response
ai_diagnostics.user_error_network: Network error while contacting the AI service
ai_diagnostics.user_error_rate_limited: The AI service rate-limited the request
ai_diagnostics.user_error_unauthorized: Unauthorized request to the AI service

files.file_lock_notice: File lock is enabled for the current WordPress site. Runtime data such as media uploads, cache, translations, and security plugin logs can still be written. Writes to core files, plugins, themes, site configuration, and executable PHP files are blocked.
files.name: Name
files.compression_busy: Compression is in progress. Large files may take a while; please wait for the completion notice.
files.confirm_fix_permissions:
Fix permissions for {{domain}}?

Directories will be set to 755, files to 644, sensitive files will be tightened, and the owner will be the site user: {{user}}.
files.current_selection: {{count}} items selected on the current page
files.decompression_busy: Decompression is in progress. Large files may take a while; please wait for the completion notice.
files.extract_overwrite_conflict:
The following files will be overwritten. Continue?

{{items}}
files.paste_target_required: Select a target website or directory first
files.remote_import_allow_insecure_tls: Allow self-signed or untrusted HTTPS certificates
files.remote_import_completed_fix_permissions: Remote import completed, but permission changes failed. Please click Fix Permissions.
files.remote_import_create_file_failed: Failed to create the remote import file: {{error}}
files.remote_import_disk_space_low: Less than 1 GB of free space remains on the target disk; download stopped
files.remote_import_filename_required: Could not infer a filename from the URL. Please enter one manually
files.remote_import_host_local: Remote URLs cannot point to the local machine
files.remote_import_host_private: Remote URLs cannot point to private network or local addresses
files.remote_import_host_resolve_failed: Failed to resolve the remote URL host
files.remote_import_read_failed: Failed to read the remote file: {{error}}
files.remote_import_rename_failed: Failed to save the remote file
files.remote_import_request_failed: Failed to create the download request
files.remote_import_save_failed: Failed to save the remote file: {{error}}
files.remote_import_too_large: Remote file exceeds the 5 GB limit
files.remote_import_too_many_redirects: Too many redirects for the remote address
files.remote_import_url_no_userinfo: Remote URLs cannot contain a username or password
files.skip_existing_prompt: Skip existing items and continue pasting the rest?
files.target_is_directory: The target path already exists and is a directory

security.rate_limit_help: When enabled, unauthenticated visitors are rate limited while logged-in WordPress users are exempt. Repeated 429 responses will be banned by Fail2ban.
security.requests_per_minute_help: Recommended 30-60 for non-Elementor heavy pages. Recommended 60-120 for Elementor or multi-plugin sites. See Help in the upper-right for tuning and testing advice.
security.burst_help: Buffer for concurrent first-screen page requests. Recommended 150-300 for Elementor or multi-plugin sites, and 30-120 for non-Elementor sites. See Help in the upper-right for tuning and testing advice.
security.burst_warning: Note: IPs that trigger 429 repeatedly will be banned automatically by Fail2ban. Leave enough buffer for normal traffic.
security.enable_bot_limit: Enable unified Bot UA rate limit
security.bot_limit_help: Disabled by default. When enabled, each site has an independent rate limit. Common Bot UAs within the same site share that site's limit bucket, while regular browsers are unaffected.
security.bot_requests_help: Default: 30. Used to suppress continuous multi-IP fake crawler scanning and avoid overloading WordPress dynamic 404 handling.
security.bot_burst_help: Default: 20. Googlebot/Bingbot are exempt only when the source IP belongs to official ranges. Fake search crawlers enter the rate limit bucket.
security.official_whitelist: Official Whitelist (pulled automatically by the panel)
security.custom_whitelist: Custom Whitelist (not overwritten by official pulls)
security.wp_security_path_help: One path per line. * wildcards are allowed. Must start with /. Regex is not supported, and spaces, semicolons, quotes, or .. are not allowed.
security.cdn_realip_conclusion: Conclusion: after CDN Real IP is enabled for a site, log analysis, abnormal access detection, and automatic banning work with the real visitor IP.
security.cdn_realip_compatible_help: If you cannot provide CDN origin IP ranges, choose Generic CDN (compatible mode). Normal CDN setups will not accidentally ban CDN nodes.
security.cdn_realip_strict_help: If you can get CDN origin IP ranges, create a group and enter them to reduce Header spoofing risk. Cloudflare is handled automatically by the system.
security.compatible_group_help: Use this mode when you cannot provide CDN origin IP ranges. It fits most regular CDN setups.
security.cloudflare_group_help: Cloudflare real IP handling is automatic globally. You do not need to select this group on site details.
security.name: Name
security.trusted_cdn_origin_ips_placeholder: One per line. Leave blank for compatible mode.
security.trusted_cdn_origin_ips_help: Leave blank if you cannot provide CDN origin IP ranges, and the site will use compatible mode. If you can obtain vendor origin IP ranges, enter them here for stricter and more reliable logging and banning.
security.description_placeholder: Describe the CDN vendor or purpose
security.header_name_help: Common CDNs in China usually use X-Forwarded-For. Header names may contain only letters, numbers, and hyphens.
security.cdn_mode_strict_trusted_ips: Strict mode: trusted origin IP ranges configured
security.cdn_mode_cloudflare_auto: Handled automatically: Cloudflare IPs are pulled automatically
security.cdn_mode_compatible_no_origin_ips: Compatible mode: use when CDN origin IP ranges are unavailable
security.cdn_mode_compatible_missing_origin_ips: Compatible mode: no trusted origin IP ranges configured
security.telemetry_help: An anonymous heartbeat is sent to stats.wp-panel.org once per day. It only contains an anonymous machine identifier (SHA256 hash of /etc/machine-id) and the panel version. It does not include IPs, domains, website information, or any sensitive data. This helps us understand how many users support WP Panel.
security.confirm_delete_cdn_group: Confirm deleting CDN config group "{{name}}"? Sites bound to this group will no longer use it.
security.telemetry_disable_confirm:
After disabling, your server installation will no longer be counted in installation statistics.

The reported data contains no sensitive information—only anonymous machine identifier (SHA256 hash) and panel version.
This is only to let us know how many users support WP Panel.

Confirm disable?

firewall.wp_suspicious_access_help: Based on local WordPress security logs and Nginx error logs. Provides evidence only and does not ban automatically.
firewall.wp_suspicious_access_cache_help: Analysis results are cached for 30 seconds. If there are more than 50 WordPress sites or disk I/O is slow, the first refresh may take longer.
firewall.file_security_events_help: Records PHP execution attempts in runtime data directories and suspicious PHP files found by scanning. Displays and alerts only; it does not ban automatically or delete files.
firewall.copy_failed_manual: Copy failed, please select the content manually
firewall.file_security_summary: File security summary: {{sites}} sites, {{files}} file events, {{access}} access events
firewall.ip_filled_notice: The IP has been filled into the manual ban box. Please choose a ban duration and click Ban.
firewall.load_wp_report_failed: Failed to load the WordPress suspicious-access analysis: {{error}}
firewall.refresh_file_security_failed: Failed to refresh file security events: {{error}}

website.reinstall_warning: ⚠ This will delete all site files, clear the database, and deploy a fresh WordPress installation. All data will be unrecoverable.
website.php_password_help: For generic PHP sites, set and save the password yourself. If it is lost, use the details page to change it and get a new password.
website.document_root_help: Choose public for apps whose entry point is public/index.php, such as Laravel. Choose project root for regular PHP apps.
website.expiry_help: Leave blank for no expiration. Alert emails are sent 14 days, 7 days, and 1 day before expiration.
website.enable_free_ssl: Request a free Let's Encrypt SSL certificate at the same time
website.clean_default_plugins: Automatically remove default plugins (Akismet + Hello Dolly)
website.remove_unused_default_themes: Remove unused default themes (Twenty Twenty-Four / Twenty Twenty-Three)
website.no_enabled_themes: No enabled themes. Add them in Extensions.
website.no_enabled_plugins: No enabled plugins. Add them in Extensions.
website.cloudflare_realip_auto: Cloudflare real IP handling is automatic. No selection is needed here.
website.non_cloudflare_cdn_notice: This is only for Alibaba Cloud ESA, Tencent EdgeOne, UpYun, or other non-Cloudflare CDNs.
website.no_non_cloudflare_cdn_groups: No selectable non-Cloudflare CDN groups. Add one in Security first.
website.document_root_detail_help: The site root is still used for file management and backups. This only changes the public Nginx root.
website.monitoring_enabled_help: Regularly checks the site's HTTP status code and sends an alert email when abnormal. Configure SMTP on the Alerts page first.
website.monitoring_disabled_help: When enabled, the panel can regularly check whether the site is reachable and email you when abnormal.
website.table_prefix_help: If the database table prefix differs from wp-config.php after migration, change it here. Click Auto Detect to read the actual prefix from the database.
website.allow_plugin_read_ssl: Allow companion plugin to read SSL certificate
website.server_level_config: Server-level configuration (pre.conf — before the root directive)
website.location_level_config: Location-level configuration (.conf — before location blocks, for cache bypass rules)
website.file_lock_help: When enabled, normal WordPress posts and media uploads still work, but plugin, theme, core file, and code directory changes are blocked.
website.debug_mode_help: When enabled, PHP errors and warnings are written to wp-content/debug.log. Enable temporarily for troubleshooting and disable during normal use.
website.post_revisions_help: Blank = WordPress default (unlimited), 0 = keep no revisions. 3-5 is recommended to reduce database usage.
website.memory_limit_help: For example, 128M or 256M. Increase it when you hit memory errors such as a blank page or 500 error.
website.xmlrpc_help: Disabled by default. When enabled, /xmlrpc.php can be accessed.
website.file_lock_enabled_notice: This site is currently locked. To save optimizations that modify wp-config.php or install/update the companion plugin, unlock it first.
website.fastcgi_cache_point_static: Nginx stores PHP page output as static HTML on disk, so visitors receive HTML directly without going through PHP/MariaDB.
website.fastcgi_cache_point_key: Each site has an independent cache version key. Clicking Clear Cache changes the key, and old cache files are removed automatically after 60 minutes.
website.fastcgi_cache_point_wp_skip: Logged-in users, POST requests, and wp-admin are automatically skipped.
website.fastcgi_cache_point_php_skip: POST requests and requests with query parameters are automatically skipped.
website.wp_optimizer_plugin_help: After the companion plugin (WP Panel Optimizer) is installed on the site, site administrators can manage cache, update checks, file editing, and other optimizations from the WordPress dashboard. These settings stay synchronized with the panel. Do not install third-party cache plugins such as WP Rocket or W3 Total Cache in Nginx mode, as they conflict with this card.
website.delete_confirm: Confirm deleting site {{domain}}? This action cannot be undone.
website.log_preview_help: This area only previews recent lines from the current log file, defaulting to the last 200 lines. Download the full daily log and rotated history logs for analysis.
website.auto_ssl_help: Automatically request a free Let's Encrypt SSL certificate. Make sure the domain resolves to this server.
website.cdn_compatible_no_origin_ips: Compatible mode: no trusted CDN origin IP ranges configured
website.cdn_header_mismatch: The selected CDN groups use different real-IP headers and cannot be saved.
website.cdn_select_non_cloudflare_help: This is only for non-Cloudflare CDNs such as Alibaba Cloud ESA, Tencent EdgeOne, and UpYun.
website.cdn_select_non_cloudflare_required: Please select at least one non-Cloudflare CDN group. Cloudflare is handled automatically by the system and does not need to be saved.
website.cdn_strict_origin_ips: Trusted CDN mode: only the selected groups' origin IP ranges are trusted
website.detect_multiple_prefixes: Multiple possible table prefixes detected: {{candidates}}. {{prefix}} has been preselected; adjust it manually if needed
website.detect_prefix_found: Table prefix detected: {{prefix}}. Click "Sync Database Info" to save it
website.file_lock_disable_confirm: After disabling file lock, the WordPress admin and file manager can modify code and site configuration again. Continue?
website.file_lock_enable_confirm: After enabling file lock, WordPress can still publish posts, upload media, generate cache, and write plugin logs, but changes to core files, plugins, themes, and site configuration will be blocked. Temporarily disable it before installing or updating plugins/themes, or before first-time security/cache setup. Continue?
website.load_current_url_failed: Failed to load the current URL: {{error}}
website.load_log_files_failed: Failed to load the log file list: {{error}}
website.log_empty_or_missing: (Log is empty or the file does not exist)
website.new_password_label: New password (leave blank to generate one automatically)
website.new_password_placeholder: Leave blank to generate a random 24-character password
website.restore_still_running: The restore task is still running. Refresh the page later to see the result.
website.ssl_manual_required: Please fill in the certificate content and private key
website.sync_wp_config_base: DB_NAME and DB_USER in wp-config.php will be overwritten with the current database name and username

settings.basic_auth_desc: First-layer authentication for the random entry path, shown as a browser login prompt
settings.password_keep: Password (at least 8 characters, leave blank to keep unchanged)
settings.web_login_desc: The web form login shown after BasicAuth passes
settings.github_proxy_help: Use this when the server cannot access GitHub directly. Leave blank to connect to GitHub directly.
settings.update_do_not_close: Do not close or refresh the page during the update.
settings.auto_update_help: Disabled by default. When enabled, updates run according to the window, release delay, and signature verification.
settings.system_update_help: Not sure what a package does? Click Details to view the official Debian package description, or copy the package name and search with a search engine or AI tool.
settings.system_update_warning: The update may take several minutes. Do not close or refresh the page.
settings.ai_settings_help: Configure the AI provider used for site failure and performance diagnostics. Run diagnostics on the AI Diagnostics page.
settings.ai_timeout_help: Recommended: 60-120 seconds. Temporarily raise it to 180 seconds for slower models.
settings.ai_privacy_notice: AI Diagnostics sends the site domain, log summary, configuration summary, and check results to the selected provider. It does not send database passwords, AI keys, SSL private keys, panel passwords, or panel database contents.
settings.wp_package_help: Local packages are preferred when creating WordPress sites. If the server cannot download WordPress online, upload the package manually here.
settings.local_package_missing: No local package is installed. Site creation will try to download it online, which may fail due to network issues.
settings.generate_password_title: Generate a random backup account password
settings.ssh_key_generated_help: The panel has generated an ed25519 key pair automatically. The public key will be embedded in the one-click command below.
settings.remote_disabled_help: When enabled, each completed backup is synchronized to the remote location automatically.
settings.remote_helper_create_help: Run the following command as root on the remote server to create the backup account and directory automatically:
settings.remote_helper_delete_help: Run as root on the remote server to delete the account and backup directory:
settings.copy_to_remote: Copy and run on the remote server:
settings.remote_helper_key_mode: · SSH key mode: the command already includes the public key, so one command completes setup
settings.remote_helper_password_mode: · Password mode: the panel installs sshpass automatically; no manual action is needed
settings.remote_helper_dedicated_user: · Use a dedicated backup account instead of root
settings.remote_helper_permission: · The backup account does not need administrator privileges, only write access to the target directory
settings.s3_help_supported: · Supports S3-compatible services such as Cloudflare R2, AWS S3, MinIO, and Backblaze B2.
settings.s3_help_bucket: · Create a Bucket in object storage and an Access Key with object read/write permissions.
settings.s3_help_region: · For Cloudflare R2, Region is usually
settings.s3_help_prefix: · Use the path prefix to distinguish different panels, for example
settings.s3_help_multipart: · Large backup files use S3 multipart upload automatically, and are still restored as one complete backup object.
settings.workflow_local_backup: The panel creates the database or file backup locally
settings.workflow_transfer: The backup file is transferred to the remote location through SSH + rsync or an S3-compatible API
settings.workflow_delete_local: If Keep Local Backups is disabled, the local file is deleted after a successful transfer to save space
settings.remote_req_ssh: SSH is enabled and reachable from this server
settings.remote_req_path: The target directory exists and the backup account has write permission
settings.remote_req_firewall: The firewall allows the SSH port (default: 22)
settings.auth_key_detail: SSH Key (recommended): the panel generates a key pair automatically, and the one-click command from the helper includes the public key. Paste and run it on the remote server. No extra software is required.
settings.auth_password_detail: Password: the panel installs sshpass automatically when saving settings. No manual action is needed.
settings.auth_s3_detail: S3-compatible object storage: uploads are signed with Access Key and Secret Key, so no remote SSH account is needed. Large files use multipart upload automatically.
settings.security_dedicated: Create a dedicated backup account on the remote server instead of using root, and keep permissions minimal
settings.security_cleanup: Remote backup directories are not cleaned automatically. Check them manually on a regular basis
settings.remote_help_tip: Tip: copy the command generated by the One-Click Setup helper to the remote server SSH terminal, paste it, and press Enter to complete the setup.
settings.panel_db_backup_help: The panel database is backed up automatically every day at 2:30 AM, keeping the latest 7 copies. The panel restarts automatically after restore.
settings.panel_db_backup_warning: ⚠ Backups contain sensitive data such as password hashes and database credentials. Do not share them. If the panel cannot start, retrieve backup files over SSH from /www/server/panel/backups/panel-db/.
settings.basic_auth_password_min_length: BasicAuth password must be at least 8 characters
settings.command_copied: Command copied. Paste into remote server as root.
settings.confirm_delete_local_wp_package: Confirm deleting the local WordPress package? The panel will automatically download it online when creating sites.
settings.confirm_restore_db_backup:
Confirm restoring from this backup?

The panel will create a safe backup first. The panel will restart automatically after restoration.
If the panel fails to start, the safety backup can be used for rollback.
settings.confirm_system_update:
Confirm running system update?

The update may take several minutes. If the page times out, do not click again—the task is still running. Duplicate clicks may cause dpkg lock conflicts.
settings.confirm_update_version: Confirm update to {{version}}? The panel may be briefly unavailable during the update.
settings.current_password_required: Current password is required to change web password
settings.new_password_min_length: New password must be at least 8 characters
settings.panel_restarting: Panel is restarting or temporarily unavailable...
settings.passwords_not_match: The two passwords do not match
settings.proxy_required: Please fill in reverse proxy address first
settings.update_completed_refresh: Update completed, panel is restarting. Auto-refresh in 3 seconds...
settings.update_status_timeout: Update status query timed out. Refresh to check panel status.
